report_metadata.report_title: PRC State-Sponsored Cyber Actor Living off the Land to Evade Detection

cves.evidence_snippet: Exploiting vulnerabilities [T1190] in widely used software including, but not limited to: CVE-2021-40539—ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus.
Exploiting vulnerabilities [T1190] in widely used software including, but not limited to: CVE-2021-27860—FatPipe WARP, IPVPN, MPVPN.

yara_rules.name: CustomFRPClient
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule CustomFRPClient { meta: description=\"Identify instances of the actor's custom FRP tool based on unique strings chosen by the actor and included in the tool\" ... }

yara_rules.name: HACKTOOL_FRPClient
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule HACKTOOL_FRPClient { meta: description=\"Identify instances of FRP tool (Note: This tool is known to be used by multiple actors, so hits would not necessarily imply activity by the specific actor described in this report)\" ... }

ttps.evidence_snippet: The actor has used the following PowerShell [T1059.001] command to identify successful logons to the host [T1033]: Get-EventLog security -instanceid 4624

The actor has used the following commands to enable port forwarding [T1090] on the host: "cmd.exe /c \"netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenaddress=0.0.0.0 listenport=9999 connectaddress=<rfc1918 internal ip address> connectport=8443 protocol=tcp\"

summary: This joint Cybersecurity Advisory (CSA) highlights a recently discovered cluster of activity associated with a People’s Republic of China (PRC) state-sponsored cyber actor, also known as Volt Typhoon. The advisory provides an overview of hunting guidance and associated best practices to detect this activity.

timeline.event: First known exploitation of CVE-2021-40539 (ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus)
timeline.evidence_snippet: Earliest evidence of exploitation of CVE-2021-40539 occurred on May 27, 2023, when threat actors began targeting ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus installations.
